AS a matter of arriving at the
approximate value of lands SUBJ
merged by waters from the ST. Joe
river, as the result of the Wash
ington Water Power Co.'s dam at
Post Falls, Idaho, the government
attorneys, appearing partially in be-
half of the indians in the hearing,
will likely introduce the testimony
of w. r. Terrell, who lives near
Occur d'Alene. Mr.. Terrell, though
perhaps realizing the small chance
he stood of being "taken up" an
nounced that he would pay 100000
for the submerged land, on which
the Indians received only about
$500 from the Washington Water
Power Co..


Post, Avery & Higgins. one of
Spokane's best known and most
able law firms, has taken up the de
Tense of the defendant concern in
the hearing. The flood caused by
the Washington Water Power Co. S
dam at Post Falls is declared to
have submerged about $3.50 acres
of land, much of which is declared
to have been very valuable. In
dams living in the flooded district
declare that the land was particu.
